proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +32.0pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Portfolio is 100% Choppy-concentrated with 3 concentration flags; if regime flips to Bear, simultaneous activation of this boost could rapidly compound exposure
R-multiple 0.61 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance per D-8; sustained low R means TAKE_SMALL cap remains essential
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +32.0pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +32.0pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, 7 days) with zero Bear exposure in 33 open positions, so activation is deferred and the existing 100% Choppy concentration is not compounded. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-14) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: UP_TRI × Metal: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, sector=Metal (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +25.5pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.72 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Sample bias possible if 17 trades cluster on few days — not verified at this layer
R-multiple 0.72 reflects D-8 forced-exit dominance; revisit if exit policy changes
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +25.5pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +25.5pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. The sector=Metal filter narrows rather than amplifies UP_TRI exposure (13 of 33 open) in a 100% Choppy-concentrated portfolio, and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental concentration. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-14) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime = Choppy (100.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime = Choppy; recommended action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until either (a) cohort tier validates to Tier M+ or (b) regime mix diversifies below 80% via natural Day-6 exits
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bull or Choppy→Bear regime flip would unwind all 33 positions simultaneously — the entire book is exposed to a single regime transition with no statistical edge support from cohort_health to justify holding through the flip.

evidence: All 33 open positions sit in Choppy regime
Matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort is Candidate tier (n=107) — unvalidated edge
Matched Choppy×DOWN_TRI cohort is Candidate tier (n=53) — unvalidated edge
Top cells UP_TRI×Choppy×IT (15.15%) and DOWN_TRI×Choppy×Energy (15.15%) below 20% but compound the regime risk
DOWN_TRI dominates 19/33 = 57.6% of signal mix, below 0.85 threshold
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until either (a) cohort tier validates to Tier M+ or (b) regime mix diversifies below 80% via natural Day-6 exits
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bull or Choppy→Bear regime flip would unwind all 33 positions simultaneously — the entire book is exposed to a single regime transition with no statistical edge support from cohort_health to justify holding through the flip.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Portfolio is 100% Choppy regime (observed 1.0, above the 0.80 threshold) and the matched Choppy×DOWN_TRI and Choppy×UP_TRI cohorts both sit only at Candidate tier (n=53 and n=107) — unvalidated edge. No single cell breaches 20% (top is UP_TRI×Choppy×IT and DOWN_TRI×Choppy×Energy both at 15.15%), but the regime concentration alone with unvalidated cohort support warrants warn — a Choppy regime flip would unwind the entire 33-position book simultaneously with no statistical justification.
